title: NOC 22233 – Construction Inspectors | GTR Immigration
description: Construction Inspectors under NOC 22233 play a critical role in ensuring structural safety, regulatory compliance, and quality assurance across infrastructure and building projects in Canada. As Canada continues investing in residential housing, highways, public infrastructure, and green building initiatives, demand for skilled Construction Inspectors remains strong under this TEER 2 occupation. For internationally trained professionals, NOC 22233 offers viable pathways under Express Entry and several Provincial Nominee Programs.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category2 – Natural and applied sciences and related occupationsTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group22 – Technical occupations related to natural and applied sciencesSub-Major Group222 – Technical occupations related to applied sciences (except engineering)Minor Group2223 – Technical inspectors and regulatory officersNOC Code22233

duties: Construction Inspectors perform some or all of the following:
Examine plans, drawings, and site layouts
Inspect buildings, bridges, dams, highways, and other infrastructure
Verify compliance with building codes and specifications
Inspect steel frameworks and concrete structures
Ensure safe working conditions on construction sites
Assess structural defects and fire hazards
Inspect residential properties and prepare condition reports
Develop and implement workplace safety compliance programs
employment_requirements: College diploma in construction, civil engineering, or architectural technology or
Several years of experience as a qualified tradesperson (e.g., plumbing, carpentry, electrical)
Provincial certification in a skilled trade or engineering technologist designation
LEED certification through the Canada Green Building Council (may be required)
Home inspector license required in Ontario, Alberta, and British Columbia
Energy advisors must register with Natural Resources Canada
Certification requirements vary by province and specialization.
salary_prospects: Annual salaries typically range from $44,300 to $96,200, depending on certification, trade background, and province.
job_outlook: Professionals with energy efficiency expertise or safety certifications may experience stronger demand.
labour_market_demand: Strong demand exists in infrastructure-heavy provinces such as Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, Saskatchewan, Quebec, and Manitoba.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 22233?
Stable employment due to strict building codes
High demand in public infrastructure projects
Opportunities in safety, compliance, and energy efficiency sectors
Canada’s construction sector remains a key economic driver, ensuring ongoing need for regulatory oversight professionals.
